translations.en: From the asceticism of Yahya ibn Zakariyya (peace be upon them both) is that he came to Bayt al-Maqdis, and he looked at the striving rabbis and monks, who wore garments of hair and hooded robes of wool. They had pierced their collarbones and threaded chains through them and tied them to the pillars of the mosque. When he saw that, he went to his mother and said: 'O mother! Weave for me a garment of hair and a hooded robe of wool so that I may go to Bayt al-Maqdis and worship Allah with the rabbis and monks.' His mother said: 'Wait until the prophet of Allah comes and I consult him.' When Zakariyya entered, she informed him. He said: 'O my son! What calls you to this when you are but a young boy?' Yahya said: 'O father! Have you not seen those younger than me who have tasted death?' He said: 'Indeed.' Then he told his mother to weave for him. She did. He dressed in the garment and went to Bayt al-Maqdis, worshiping Allah with the rabbis until the garment consumed his flesh. One day he looked at what had wasted from his body and wept. Allah revealed to him: 'O Yahya! Are you crying over what has wasted from your body? By My might and My glory! If you were to glimpse the Fire, you would have worn iron, let alone woven cloth.'
